title: Parser fails on `Checks` blocks (DBML v5.0.0 check definitions) emitted by sql2dbml
author: frenzzy
body:
## Problem

`dbml-renderer` cannot parse the `Checks { ... }` block that `@dbml/cli sql2dbml` emits when converting a PostgreSQL schema containing `CHECK` constraints. `Checks` is part of the official DBML spec since v5.0.0 ([check definition](https://dbml.dbdiagram.io/docs/#check-definition)), but the renderer's grammar (`src/dbml.pegjs`) has no rule for it, so rendering aborts.

## Reproduction

`schema.dbml`:

```dbml
Table "display_number_counter" {
  "tenant_scope" text [pk, not null]
  "next_num" integer [not null, default: 1]

  Checks {
    `(id = 1)` [name: 'display_number_counter_singleton']
  }
}
```

```sh
npx @softwaretechnik/dbml-renderer -i schema.dbml -o schema.svg
```

## Actual

```
Could not parse input at line 6:5. Expected "'", "'''", "\"", or whitespace but "`" found.
```

Line 6 column 5 is the backtick that wraps the check expression. The grammar's `TableItem` only accepts `Column`, `Indices`, and options, so the parser has no production for the `Checks` block and fails at the first backtick.

## Expected

`Checks` blocks parse successfully and the table renders. As with index expression details, the check constraints can be parsed and ignored visually — they are constraint metadata, not graph edges.

## Context

This breaks any pipeline that renders `sql2dbml` output for Postgres schemas containing `CHECK` constraints — e.g. singleton guards `CHECK (id = 1)` or discriminated-union guards `CHECK (num_nonnulls(a, b) = 1)`. Both `@softwaretechnik/dbml-renderer@1.0.31` and the `@frenzzy/dbml-renderer` fork (before the fix below) reproduce it.

## References

- DBML check definition: https://dbml.dbdiagram.io/docs/#check-definition
- `@dbml/core` check support: https://github.com/holistics/dbml/issues/484 (PR #765, released in v5.0.0)

title: Support quoted table names with spaces.
author: rsanders-relias
body:
While it's discouraged in most SQL sources, there are a few non-standard relational stores where tables have "human friendly" names which is typically paired with some type of opaque backend identified such as a uuid or other. 

Many DBML tools support quoting table names such as "Document Library" (see the VS Code plugin).  This works well as in the case mentioned above, the backend ids aren't usable labels.

Sample would be:
```
Table "Document Library" {
  "Document Id" text [pk, unique]
  "Created On" timestamp
  "Modified On" timestamp
  Title text
}
```

I was hoping to auto-generate some basic diagrams for some of the data stores, but dbml-renderer errors out on the naming.  I could certainly rename things in the DMBL file, however that fails to capture the real label/name in the system.

title: Feature request: support for TablePartials
author: peterhoeg

title: Feature request: custom theming
author: yinchi
body:
It would be nice to be able to set the edge and text color. Currently, trying to embed a DBML diagram on any dark-mode webpage makes the blue relationship information text almost disappear.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/98eef1eb-b8e8-44a6-a3ae-84ab684bae16)

Perhaps the syntax can be like:

```
Theme {
    background transparent
    table_title 000066
    table_title_text white
    edge orange
    edge_text red
}
```

plus any other parameters that are used to set up color in the intermediate .dot files.

Alternatively, a set of built-in themes such as "light" and "dark" would also work.

title: Bigger diagrams cant be rendered?

body: Could a zoom option be added?

title: Inline references should be case-insensitive
author: brunobcosta
body:
## Context
Some editors like https://dbdiagram.io/ and vs-code plugins suggest the "Ref" keyword like this, in pascal-case.
I couldn't find any mention in the dbml spec about keyword casing, so my guess is that this should be case-insensitive, just like others like "pk" and "table" already are.


## Problem
Inline references are not rendering when has capital letters in keyword.
In the following example, five references are expected in the output, but just three has actually created. The capital two arent recognized.
```dbml

Table countries {
  code int [pk]
  name varchar
}

Table users as U {
  id int [pk, increment]
  full_name varchar

  country_code int [ref: > countries.code] //has output
}

Table merchants {
  id int [pk]
  name varchar
  country_code int [Ref: > countries.code] //does not output
  admin_id int [REF: > U.id] //does not output
}

Table products {
  id int [pk]
  name varchar
  merchant_id int 
  country_code int
}

ref: products.merchant_id > merchants.id //has output
Ref: products.country_code > countries.code //has output
```

title: Feature request: Option to show only PK/FK/Table
author: benjdv
body:
Hi,

Some tables have a lot of columns and the rendering becomes difficult to understand.
Options to limit what is drawn (for exemple only PK/FK/Table) could provide more readability in a number of cases.

with additional information like "x other fields remaining"

Regards,

title: bug: SyntaxError: Unexpected token '.'
author: skramm
body:
Hi,
Seems like a nice tool, unfortunately I am unable to make it work.
I installed it in a Ubuntu 22 docker container, installed npm , dit this:
```
npm install -g @softwaretechnik/dbml-renderer
```
But trying to use it with this `test.dbml` file:
```
Table users {
  id integer
  username varchar
  role varchar
  created_at timestamp
}
```
produces this:

```
# dbml-renderer -i test.dbml -o test.svg
/usr/local/lib/node_modules/@softwaretechnik/dbml-renderer/lib/renderer.js:32
        const tableColor = !this.table.settings?.headercolor
                                                ^

SyntaxError: Unexpected token '.'
    at wrapSafe (internal/modules/cjs/loader.js:915:16)
    at Module._compile (internal/modules/cjs/loader.js:963:27)
    at Object.Module._extensions..js (internal/modules/cjs/loader.js:1027:10)
    at Module.load (internal/modules/cjs/loader.js:863:32)
    at Function.Module._load (internal/modules/cjs/loader.js:708:14)
    at Module.require (internal/modules/cjs/loader.js:887:19)
    at require (internal/modules/cjs/helpers.js:74:18)
    at Object.<anonymous> (/usr/local/lib/node_modules/@softwaretechnik/dbml-renderer/lib/api.js:6:20)
    at Module._compile (internal/modules/cjs/loader.js:999:30)
    at Object.Module._extensions..js (internal/modules/cjs/loader.js:1027:10)
```
Additional info:
```
# npm --version
8.5.1
# nodejs --version
v12.22.9
```
To me, seems that this is a spurious '?', can you confirm (and removed, if so).

title: Error using decimal(10,2)
author: danilort
body:
If I use the data_type decima(10,2), I get error:

`Could not parse input at line 5:22. Expected ")" or [a-zA-Z0-9_] but "," found.`

# file 4.dbml

```
Table utenti {
    id int [pk, increment]
    nome varchar     
    data_creato timestamp
    numero decimal(10,2)
    nazione_id int
}

```

title: Blank after "]", generates error
author: danilort
body:
Hello,
when **after a closing square bracket**, there are one or more blanks space, I get an error.

# file 1.dbml
```
Table nazione {
    id int [pk]
    nome varchar
    continente int
}
```
(no blank space after [pk])

# file 2.dbml
```
Table nazione {
    id int [pk]                 
    nome varchar
    continente int
}
```

(some blank space after [pk])


# output
![immagine](https://github.com/softwaretechnik-berlin/dbml-renderer/assets/16148184/b49b8ab0-04f8-4bad-827a-dc2393d7cc3f)

### EXAMPLES
`id int [pk, increment]`

> OK

`id int [pk, increment]                 `

> Error

`id int [pk, increment]      //               `

> OK

`nome varchar             `
>OK

title: Add support for array fields
author: thgh
body:
Not sure if Prisma specific, but the dbml generator creates a structure like this:
```dbml
Table example {
  ...
  algorithms Algorithm[] [not null, note: 'List of algos']
}
```

title: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'start')
author: ahuffman
body:
Getting error when attempting to render a dbml file:
`Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'start')`

Running the command:
`dbml-renderer -i docs/db2.dbml -o dbdrawing.svg -f svg`

The dbml file looks like this:
```
 Project simplebank {
     database_type: 'PostgreSQL'
     Note: '''
       # Simple Bank Database
     '''
 }

Table accounts as A {
  id bigserial [pk]
  owner varchar [ref: > U.username, not null]
  balance bigint [not null]
  currency varchar [not null]
  created_at timestamptz [not null, default: `now()`]

  Indexes {
    owner
    // to make it so an owner can only have different accounts with different currency
    (owner, currency) [unique]
  }
}

Table entries {
  id bigserial [pk]
  account_id bigint [ref: > A.id, not null]
  amount bigint [not null]
  created_at timestamptz [not null, default: `now()`]

  Indexes {
    account_id
  }
}

Table transfers {
  id bigserial [pk]
  from_account_id bigint [ref: > A.id, not null]
  to_account_id bigint [ref: > A.id, not null]
  amount bigint [not null]
  created_at timestamptz [not null, default: `now()`]

  Indexes {
    from_account_id
    to_account_id
    (from_account_id, to_account_id)
  }
}

Table users as U {
  username varchar [pk]
  hased_password varchar [not null]
  full_name varchar [not null]
  email varchar [unique, not null]
  // so we can enforce password age policies
  password_changed_at timestamptz [not null, default: '0001-01-01 00:00:00+00']
  created_at timestamptz [not null, default: `now()`]
}

Table sessions {
  id uuid [pk]
  username varchar [ref: > U.username, not null]
  refresh_token varchar [not null]
  user_agent varchar [not null]
  client_ip varchar [not null]
  is_blocked boolean [not null, default: false]
  expires_at timestamptz [not null]
  created_at timestamptz [not null, default: `now()`]
}
```

Additional info:
Running on MacOS Ventura 13.3.1 (a)
Node version:
```
node -v
v20.1.0
```

title: Suggestion: Run in-browser rather than command line
author: abdulhakam
body: As you see [this project](https://github.com/TruDan/dbdiagram-oss) is similar but has not been updated from quite long and is quite good and its all in browser(although it doesn't have export functionality). And here the output much organized and beautiful than that one and I see that most of the code is written in javascript, Is It possible to convert the whole process to run in-browser so that it can be run as an offline webpage, like select a dbml file in browser and processes it via javascript and saves it as a download file?
